    "resolution": "backport",

        "package": "glib2",
        "patch_url": "https://gitlab.gnome.org/GNOME/glib/-/commit/b94b44407a088e6e8278d9db8b59fb377e84bda4.patch",
        "justification": "The patch fixes a serial number overflow issue in GDBusConnection by checking if the serial counter is at its maximum value.         "jira_issue": "RHEL-114059",

        "fix_version": "rhel-9.8"
    }
}

Output from Triage Agent: 

*Resolution*: backport
*Patch URL*: https://gitlab.gnome.org/GNOME/glib/-/commit/b94b44407a088e6e8278d9db8b59fb377e84bda4.patch
*Justification*: The patch fixes a serial number overflow issue in GDBusConnection by checking if the serial counter is at its maximum value. If it is, the patch resets the counter to 0, which after incrementing becomes 1, effectively preventing the serial number from becoming 0 and causing a crash.
*Fix Version*: rhel-9.7.z

This ticket is currently in an out-of-process state because it moved to "Integration/Release Pending" status but without a "Errata Link": the code here worked on is likely to not have landed in advisory, or perhaps it was, but in a later build/ticket. Was the advisory dropped? Or was this ticket manually moved to "Integration/Release Pending" without there being an advisory?
Please evaluate the situation of this ticket and move it to a better state: either to Closed with an appropriate resolution (e.g. Obsolete) or back to "In Progress" or "Planning", as you see fit. Or if you're sure that you followed the process strictly, please ignore this comment.
